rationale: Zauberman et al. (2009) is cited as key prior evidence that subjective time perception is linked to intertemporal preferences, and the citing paper explicitly positions itself as contributing to that research stream. The citation is also used in hypothesis development to justify why perceived interval length should affect discount rates and choice. Thus, the cited paper plays a meaningful conceptual role in framing and prediction, not just a background mention.
relation_description: The citing paper extends Zauberman et al. (2009) by adding endpoint valence (anticipated gains vs losses) as an antecedent of subjective time perception. It then tests how this altered subjective time feeds into planned consumption and intertemporal discounting outcomes.
